feed.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage

entry.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
entry.summary: The email primarily challenges the recipient to provide technical arguments against a proposal that emphasizes data movement based on consent rather than coercion. It highlights a clear stance against using force or pressure in data handling practices, advocating for a more ethical approach where user permission is central. The sender expresses strong disapproval of any support for coercive methods and criticizes the recipient's previous response as inadequate and dismissive. This insistence on ethical standards in data management underscores the importance of respecting user autonomy and the need for substantive, technically sound discussions when addressing such topics.
